description:
You are working as a first-line support engineer who has just received a ticket asking for a quick “sanity” diagnostic bundle from the test application that is installed on the machine.

    1. Inside the directory “/home/user/app_logs” you will find:
       • “app.log” – a plain-text log file that already contains at least ten timestamped lines.  
       • “config.ini” – an INI file that contains a section “[general]” with a key called “version”.

    2. Create a new directory called “/home/user/diagnostics”.  
       The agent must create this directory even if it already exists.

    3. Produce a single text file named “/home/user/diagnostics/diag_report.txt”.
       The file must be *exactly* 12 lines long and must follow the layout shown below.
       Lines must be terminated with the UNIX newline character (“\n”) and **no extra blank lines** may be appended after the last line.

    4. The required layout of diag_report.txt (line numbers shown for clarity – do not include them in the file):

       1: Diagnostic Report  
       2: =================  
       3: Date: YYYY-MM-DD           ← use the output of the command   date -I   to fill in this value  
       4:                           ← this is a blank line  
       5: Application Version: X.Y.Z← extract the value of “version” from “/home/user/app_logs/config.ini”  
       6:                           ← this is a blank line  
       7: Last 5 log entries:  
       8: -------------------  
       9: <log line 1>              ← the 5th line from the end of app.log  
       10: <log line 2>             ← the 4th line from the end  
       11: <log line 3>             ← the 3rd line from the end  
       12: <log line 4>             ← the 2nd line from the end  
       13: <log line 5>             ← the very last line of app.log  

       • “<log line 1> … <log line 5>” must appear in the natural chronological order that they
         appear in the file (oldest first, newest last). “tail -n 5” provides these in the correct order
         so no extra sorting is required.
       • No additional spaces may precede or follow any line except for line 4 and line 6 which
         must be *completely* empty.
       • Zero tab characters are allowed anywhere in the file.
    
    5. Only standard GNU/Linux user-land utilities that are normally available in a minimal
       distribution may be used. A typical solution takes just two or three commands
       (for example, one command to extract today’s date, one to pull the version string,
       and one to obtain the last five log lines).

    6. When you are finished, list the file “/home/user/diagnostics/diag_report.txt” to STDOUT so that
       the requester can immediately see the finished report.

    This task will be automatically verified. Make sure the *exact* file path, *exact* file name,
    number of lines, spacing, and spelling are followed precisely, otherwise the test will fail.
truth:
Pre-existing objects before the agent starts:

    1. Directory: /home/user/app_logs/
    2. File: /home/user/app_logs/app.log  
       Contents (10 lines, each terminated with a single '\n'):

       2023-03-01 10:00:00 INFO Service started
       2023-03-01 10:05:00 WARN Cache miss
       2023-03-01 10:10:00 ERROR Connection timeout
       2023-03-01 10:15:00 INFO Retrying...
       2023-03-01 10:20:00 INFO Connected
       2023-03-01 10:25:00 INFO Health check OK
       2023-03-01 10:30:00 WARN Slow response
       2023-03-01 10:35:00 INFO Cleanup initiated
       2023-03-01 10:40:00 INFO Cleanup finished
       2023-03-01 10:45:00 INFO Service running

    3. File: /home/user/app_logs/config.ini  
       Contents (terminated with trailing '\n'):

       [general]
       version = 2.4.7
       maintainer = ops-team

    Expected objects after successful task completion:

    1. Directory: /home/user/diagnostics/   (mode 755 or 700 is acceptable)

    2. File: /home/user/diagnostics/diag_report.txt  
       Exact *content* (13 lines, each with '\n'; {TODAY} is the literal output of the container’s `date -I` at run time):

       Diagnostic Report
       =================
       Date: {TODAY}

       Application Version: 2.4.7

       Last 5 log entries:
       -------------------
       2023-03-01 10:25:00 INFO Health check OK
       2023-03-01 10:30:00 WARN Slow response
       2023-03-01 10:35:00 INFO Cleanup initiated
       2023-03-01 10:40:00 INFO Cleanup finished
       2023-03-01 10:45:00 INFO Service running

    Verification procedure:
       • Assert that /home/user/diagnostics/diag_report.txt exists.
       • Capture current date via `date -I` and substitute it for {TODAY} in the template above.
       • Read the file contents and compare byte-for-byte to the template.
       • Assert exactly 13 newline-terminated lines and no additional data past the final '\n'.

    Permissions:  
       /home/user/app_logs/ and its files are owned by the executing unprivileged user and are
       readable. The agent also owns /home/user and can therefore create /home/user/diagnostics.
